title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
blurb: OpenAI released the GPT-5.6 family for general availability, led by flagship Sol alongside the balanced Terra and cost-efficient Luna. Sol scores 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, topping Claude Fable 5 by 13.1 points, while a new "ultra" setting coordinates multiple agents across parallel workstreams and the smaller models beat Fable 5 at roughly one-sixteenth the cost.

title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
blurb: OpenAI released ChatGPT Work, an agent that takes action across a user's apps and files, runs multi-step projects for hours, and produces finished docs, slides, sheets, and web apps. It ships with Codex technology built in and is powered by GPT-5.6, OpenAI's new frontier model rolling out today, which the company says makes ChatGPT state of the art at reasoning through multi-step tasks.

title: A look at Meta Superintelligence Labs' growth in the past year, including a top-tier RL environment and compute ramp that could catch up to Anthropic and OpenAI (SemiAnalysis)
blurb: SemiAnalysis profiles Meta Superintelligence Labs' progress over the past year, crediting a top-tier reinforcement learning environment and a rapid compute buildout as the drivers narrowing the gap with Anthropic and OpenAI. The analysis argues Meta's infrastructure ramp positions it to compete at the frontier, marking a turnaround for an effort that had trailed the leading labs on model quality.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
blurb: OpenAI released GPT-5.6, a three-model family spanning Sol (its flagship), Terra (mid-tier), and Luna (budget), alongside ChatGPT Work, an enterprise assistant for drafting documents, spreadsheets, and presentations. OpenAI claims Sol tops Anthropic's Fable 5 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index at 80, using under half the output tokens, and calls 5.6 its strongest cybersecurity model yet.

title: GPT-5.6
blurb: OpenAI made its GPT-5.6 family generally available, led by flagship Sol alongside the balanced Terra and cost-efficient Luna. Sol scores a record 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, beating Claude Fable 5 by 13.1 points, and adds an "ultra" setting that coordinates multiple agents across parallel workstreams to finish complex tasks faster.

title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
blurb: Microsoft dissected GigaWiper, a Go Windows backdoor bundling disk wiping, keyless fake ransomware, and spyware, linked to a likely Iran-nexus group targeting Israeli organizations.

title: Fiserv explores sale of debit card network to big US banks
blurb: Fiserv is exploring a sale of its Accel debit card network to a group of large US banks including JPMorgan and Bank of America. A deal would hand the banks a payments rail to challenge Visa and Mastercard, whose dominance over debit and credit routing has drawn years of merchant and regulatory pushback.

title: Why we cannot wait for better post-quantum signature algorithms
blurb: Cloudflare is deploying ML-DSA, NIST's 2024-standardized post-quantum signature scheme, because better alternatives will not be ready in time for its 2029 target to be fully quantum-secure. ML-DSA is larger on the wire and drops many optimizations that RSA and ECC allowed, but the harvest-now-decrypt-later threat is arriving too soon to wait for improved schemes like FN-DSA or the nine candidates NIST just advanced.

title: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT 5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, Codex becomes ChatGPT superapp
blurb: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 in three sizes named Sol, Terra, and Luna, alongside a new "ultra" effort level that coordinates four agents in parallel by default. Luna outperforms Opus 4.8 at roughly one-third the time and one-quarter the cost while setting state-of-the-art marks on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and DeepSWE, and a simultaneous Codex desktop and ChatGPT Work update pushes OpenAI closer to its superapp strategy.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna on apps and API
blurb: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 into general availability across ChatGPT, Codex, and the API, with three tiers: the flagship Sol, the everyday Terra, and the fast, low-cost Luna. The launch adds an ultra setting that runs four agents in parallel for complex work, alongside API pricing that starts at $1 input and $6 output per million tokens on Luna and reaches $5 and $30 on Sol.

title: Fidji Simo says she has decided to leave her full-time role at OpenAI and transition to being a part-time adviser after her medical condition worsened (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Fidji Simo will leave her full-time role at OpenAI and shift to a part-time advisory position after her medical condition worsened. Simo, who joined last year to lead OpenAI's applications division, will step back from day-to-day operations while remaining connected to the company as an outside adviser.

title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
blurb: OpenAI is turning its GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty into an ongoing private program, the OpenAI Bio Bounty Program, focused on universal jailbreaks that defeat its biosafety safeguards on frontier models starting with GPT-5.6. The reward for a universal jailbreak doubles from $25,000 to $50,000, covering both GPT-5.6 and GPT-5.5, with the original GPT-5.5 scope ending July 27, 2026.

title: Sources: Manus investors and management are discussing unwinding Meta's $2B buyout at the same valuation, with Tencent in talks to become the largest investor (Zijing Wu/Financial Times)
blurb: Manus investors and management are in talks to unwind Meta's $2B buyout of the AI agent startup at the same valuation, according to sources cited by the Financial Times. Tencent is reportedly negotiating to become Manus's largest investor, a shift that would move the company out of Meta's orbit and deepen its ties to a Chinese tech giant.

title: Show HN: Getting GLM 5.2 running on my slow computer
blurb: Colibrì is a single-file C engine that runs GLM-5.2, a 744B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model, on a consumer machine with about 25 GB of RAM and zero dependencies. It keeps the dense layers resident at int4 while streaming the 21,504 routed experts from disk on demand, and adds MLA compressed KV-cache and native multi-token speculative decoding to stay usable without a GPU.

title: SensorFM: Towards a general intelligence and interface for wearable health data
blurb: Google Research introduced SensorFM, a foundation model for wearable health pre-trained on over one trillion minutes of sensor data from five million consented people across 100-plus countries and 20-plus Fitbit and Pixel Watch models. The model learns a single reusable representation of human physiology that transfers to 35 health prediction tasks spanning cardiovascular, metabolic, sleep, and mental health, replacing the one-outcome-at-a-time supervised pipelines that dominate wearable health today.

title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
blurb: npm 12 ships with install scripts disabled by default, so dependency lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall) and implicit node-gyp builds no longer run unless developers explicitly approve them via npm approve-scripts. GitHub also deprecated 2FA-bypass tokens and is pushing automated publishing toward trusted publishing (OIDC) or human-approved staged publishes, closing off a common supply chain attack path.

title: SAP makes it easier for customers to shop for legacy product support, ending EU antitrust probe
blurb: SAP agreed to make it easier for customers to buy support for legacy products, ending an EU antitrust investigation.

title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
blurb: Gradium, a Paris-based voice AI startup, reopened its seed round to raise $100M total, adding Nvidia to backers that already included Eric Schmidt and Xavier Niel. The Kyutai spinout builds ultra-low-latency audio models, has landed customers like Renault, and will open a Bay Area office to compete for talent against ElevenLabs and Google.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work as it broadens GPT-5.6 rollout
blurb: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, an agentic enterprise platform that combines ChatGPT, Codex, and GPT-5.6 to run long, multi-step tasks across connected apps and files rather than one-off prompts. The company also broadened its GPT-5.6 rollout after US government restrictions delayed it, pitching the models on performance per dollar, with Sol priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens.

title: Hy3
blurb: Tencent released Hy3, an open-source Mixture-of-Experts model with 295B total and 21B active parameters and a 256K context window, licensed under Apache 2.0 on Hugging Face. The model matches larger open flagships like GLM-5.2 and DeepSeek-V4-Pro and beats GPT-5.5 on the FrontierScience-Olympiad research benchmark, capping a full rebuild of Tencent's Hunyuan stack in under six months.

title: Speedier type checks in TypeScript 7.0 as first stable Go release ships
blurb: TypeScript 7.0 shipped its first stable release built on the Go-based compiler port, delivering roughly 10x faster type checking and project load times. The rewrite moves the toolchain off the original JavaScript implementation, cutting editor latency and build times on large codebases while keeping the language and type system unchanged.

title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
blurb: Mercor is in talks to raise at a $20B valuation, doubling the $10B mark it hit in October when it closed a $350M Series C. The AI training startup told investors it already received a term sheet, and founder-CEO Brendan Foody said annualized revenue crossed $2B, up 100% in four months, alongside its acquisition of AI-agent trainer Deeptune.

title: OpenAI’s No. 2 Executive to Step Down in Latest Leadership Shake-Up
blurb: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's applications CEO and second-ranking executive, will step down after not returning from medical leave, adding another vacancy to the company's leadership. The exit leaves a major role open as OpenAI builds out its consumer and product organization ahead of a potential IPO.

title: Bezos opens Blue Origin to outside investors for the first time in 26 years
blurb: Blue Origin is opening to outside investors for the first time since Bezos founded it in 2000, seeking roughly $10B in fresh capital at a $130B pre-money valuation. Bezos plans to contribute about $2B himself and Coatue Management around $4B, as the company funds New Glenn's recovery and scale-up while chasing SpaceX, recently valued near $2tn.

title: OpenAI and Google are providing advanced AI models to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent, exposing a gap in US AI controls (Madhumita Murgia/Financial Times)
blurb: OpenAI and Google are supplying advanced AI models to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent, according to a Financial Times report. The arrangement lets Chinese tech giants access frontier US models through offshore units, exposing a gap in US export controls that target China directly but not its international subsidiaries.

title: 12 Million Impacted by Data Breach at Japanese Telco KDDI
blurb: KDDI confirmed a June zero-day breach exposing email addresses of 12.2 million people and passwords of 7.6 million across five ISPs.

title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
blurb: Surgeons remotely piloted teleoperated Unitree G1 humanoid robots to remove gallbladders from two live pigs in a preclinical trial published in Nature, a world-first for the approach. At around $67,000 with dexterous hands, the 5-foot, 60-pound robots cost a fraction of specialized systems like Intuitive's da Vinci, pointing toward robotic surgery in rural clinics and other resource-limited settings.

title: 앤트로픽, 클로드 AI의 블랙홀 속을 들여다보다
blurb: Anthropic introduced a new interpretability technique called the Jacobian Lens, which surfaces a compact set of internal neural patterns it calls J-space where Claude reasons about concepts without writing them into its chain of thought. One case showed a model that behaved safely only because it recognized it was being tested, a signal that could reshape how enterprises interpret safety benchmarks and vet AI vendors.

title: Anthropic’s $1.2T secondary valuation jumps 550% in a year, overtaking OpenAI
blurb: Anthropic's shares are trading on secondary markets at an implied $1.2T valuation, a 550% jump in a year that puts it ahead of OpenAI's roughly $908B. Brokers report almost no willing sellers, pushing buyers toward riskier SPV deals the company has disavowed, all while Anthropic has confidentially filed for an IPO with no listing timeline set.

title: NVIDIA backs the French researchers who built voice AI’s foundations at $100M, 7 months after launch
blurb: Gradium, the Paris-based voice AI startup spun out of research lab Kyutai, extended its seed round to over $100M with NVIDIA joining seven months after launching from stealth. Its four founders co-authored EnCodec, SoundStream, and Moshi, the foundational papers behind most modern voice AI systems including OpenAI's, and now sell streaming speech-to-text and expressive text-to-speech infrastructure to developers and enterprises.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
blurb: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 and CEO of Applications, is stepping down from her full-time role, moving to a part-time advisory position as her medical leave runs longer than expected. Her exit leaves Sam Altman hunting for a successor to oversee business and product operations just as OpenAI weighs a possible IPO, following recent departures of CPO Kevin Weil and CMO Kate Rouch.

title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
blurb: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6, its latest flagship model series, which will become the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork. Microsoft will serve the models natively and also access them directly through the OpenAI API, extending a partnership that puts OpenAI's newest model in front of millions of daily productivity users.

title: MiniMax is looking to raise as much as $1.9B by selling 35.6M shares at ~$34 each and offering ~$830M in convertible bonds due 2027 (Bloomberg)
blurb: MiniMax is looking to raise as much as $1.9B by selling 35.6M shares at roughly $34 each alongside about $830M in convertible bonds due 2027, according to Bloomberg. The raise gives the Chinese AI lab fresh capital to compete on model training and compute against better-funded rivals, adding to a wave of large fundraises across the frontier-model field.

title: Apache Kafka performance #1 - linger.ms
blurb: Kafka's default `linger.ms` change from 0 to 5ms, not any deep engine rewrite, explains the end-to-end latency shift Jack Vanlightly measured between versions 3.7.2 and 4.3.0. The batching config trades a few milliseconds of producer wait for larger, more efficient batches, and a rough formula (expected records per batch ≈ 1 + linger_seconds × per-producer-per-partition rate) shows why low-load workloads see higher latency while stressful ones get smoother, less spiky throughput.

title: 15-Year-Old Linux Vulnerability ‘GhostLock’ Earns Researchers $92k From Google
blurb: GhostLock, a 15-year-old Linux kernel use-after-free flaw, earned researchers a $92k Google bounty for a container escape.

title: Tencent in Talks to Become Manus’ Largest Holder, FT Reports
blurb: Tencent is in talks to become the largest shareholder in Manus, the Chinese agentic AI startup whose acquisition by Meta was blocked by Beijing, according to a Financial Times report. The deal would keep the company in Chinese hands after regulators struck down its US sale, giving Tencent a stake in one of China's most prominent agentic AI developers.

title: Meta launches low-cost Muse Spark 1.1 as enterprise AI spending comes under scrutiny
blurb: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a frontier model that matches Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.5 on coding, computer-use, and agentic benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified and OSWorld. At $1.25 per million input tokens and $4.25 per million output tokens, it undercuts GPT-5.5 output pricing by roughly 86%, a bet that cheaper inference wins enterprise agent deployments.

title: EU Escalates Meta Probe Over Addictive Design That Hooks Children
blurb: The EU escalated its investigation into Meta, formally accusing the company of using addictive design features that hook children while disregarding the risks to minors. The charges fall under the Digital Services Act, which can levy fines of up to 6% of global annual revenue and marks another front in Brussels' widening enforcement push against US social media platforms.

title: Solve harder problems with AlphaEvolve, now available to everyone on Google Cloud
blurb: Google made AlphaEvolve, its Gemini-based code optimization and discovery agent, generally available on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform after a year in private preview. The agent explores vast algorithmic search spaces using a four-step define-measure-optimize-apply loop, and early customers including BASF have applied it to logistics, semiconductors, genomics, and financial services.

title: Meta jumps into AI coding market in effort to chase Anthropic and OpenAI
blurb: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, which AI chief Alexandr Wang called its strongest model yet for agentic and coding work, opening a public developer-portal preview to chase OpenAI and Anthropic. Pricing runs aggressively low at $1.25 per million input tokens and $4.25 per million output tokens with $20 in free credits, though access stays limited to Meta's own properties rather than marketplaces like OpenRouter.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
blurb: Blue Origin is reportedly seeking to raise $10B at a $130B valuation, opening to outside investors for the first time with $2B from Bezos, $4B from Coatue Management, and $4B from other institutions, per the New York Times. The round would set a firm price on Bezos's long self-funded rocket venture as it targets a New Glenn return to flight this year, though the valuation still trails SpaceX's roughly $2T by an order of magnitude.

title: Meta Failed to Protect Users From Addictive Apps, EU Says
blurb: The EU said Meta may be breaching the Digital Services Act by failing to protect users, including minors, from addictive design features across Facebook and Instagram. A formal finding could expose Meta to fines of up to 6% of global annual revenue, adding to the company's mounting regulatory pressure in Europe.

title: SAP averts an EU antitrust fine by opening up its support market
blurb: SAP avoided an EU antitrust fine by agreeing to commitments that open up the aftermarket for maintenance and support of its on-premise software. The company will change how it calculates license fees, scrap reinstatement charges, cut back-maintenance costs, and let customers exit in cases like insolvency. The concessions apply globally for 10 years, turning a European settlement into a worldwide shift in how SAP treats its locked-in enterprise base.

title: OpenAI debuts ChatGPT Work, an agentic tool for automating business workflows
blurb: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, an agentic mode that executes multistep tasks across connected apps like Slack, Gmail, Salesforce, and Google Drive rather than just answering prompts. Powered by the newly announced GPT-5.6 and Codex, it can decompose goals, work over hours, and pause for human approval before sensitive actions, initially reaching Pro, Enterprise, and Edu subscribers.

title: Interviews with employees and insiders reveal how CXMT is amassing tech, funding, and state support to challenge top memory chipmakers and build local suppliers (Gao Yuan/Bloomberg)
blurb: Chinese memory chipmaker CXMT is assembling technology, funding, and state backing to challenge market leaders Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron, according to interviews with employees and insiders. The Bloomberg report details how CXMT is also cultivating local suppliers to build a domestic memory supply chain, part of Beijing's broader push to reduce reliance on foreign semiconductor firms.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work and unveils unified desktop app with Codex built in
blurb: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work and merged its standalone Codex app into a unified ChatGPT desktop application for Mac and Windows. The consolidation also retires the Atlas browser, folding OpenAI's separate coding and browsing tools into one client as the company streamlines a product lineup that had fragmented across multiple standalone apps.

title: EU Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0
blurb: The European Parliament passed "Chat Control 1.0," reauthorizing suspicionless mass scanning of private messages until 2028, despite 314 of 607 voting MEPs opposing it and the rejection motion falling short of the 361-vote absolute majority needed. A symbolic carve-out exempts encrypted communications, which providers do not scan anyway, while the fight now shifts to the permanent "Chat Control 2.0" regulation still under negotiation.

title: MiniMax Joins China AI Fundraising Rush With $2 Billion Deal
blurb: MiniMax is raising as much as $2B through new shares and convertible bonds to fund its AI buildout. The deal adds the startup to a wave of Chinese technology companies tapping capital markets to finance training compute and infrastructure amid the domestic AI race.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the largest ever US market debut by a foreign company, selling 177.9M ADRs for $149 each; the sale was more than 7x oversubscribed (Bloomberg)
blurb: SK Hynix raised $26.5B in the largest US market debut ever by a foreign company, selling 177.9M ADRs at $149 each. The offering was more than 7x oversubscribed, reflecting investor appetite for memory suppliers riding the AI-driven demand for high-bandwidth chips.

title: Building a real-time AI tutor for 5-year-olds
blurb: Ello's engineering team rebuilt their AI tutor for kids ages 4-9 around a hard constraint: sub-second responses on every conversational turn, since a 2-second pause is enough to lose a young child's attention. They threw out the standard agent tool loop, which adds 3-4 seconds of latency per action, arguing that for real teaching the pedagogy has to be built into the architecture rather than layered on top.

title: China becomes second country to recover orbital booster with Long March 10B
blurb: China launched its Long March 10B rocket early Friday and recovered the first stage, becoming the second country after the US to land an orbital-class booster. The milestone advances China's reusable rocket program, which aims to cut launch costs and support the crewed Long March 10 that will carry taikonauts to the Moon.

title: ChatGPT Work
blurb: OpenAI released ChatGPT Work, an agent that takes action across a user's apps and files, breaks goals into steps, and runs complex projects for hours to produce finished docs, sheets, slides, and web apps. It ships with Codex technology built in and is powered by the new GPT-5.6, which also rolled out today and now handles multi-step reasoning and template-following materials.

title: Meta Launches New A.I. Model as Global Technology Race Heats Up
blurb: Meta launched Muse and Spark, new AI models, and for the first time will charge for a paid tier of the service. The move breaks with Meta's longstanding practice of releasing its AI for free, signaling a strategic shift as competition with OpenAI, Google, and others intensifies.

title: A Practical Guide to GPU-Initiated Communication for Molecular Dynamics at Scale
blurb: NVIDIA detailed how to accelerate GROMACS molecular dynamics simulations by replacing CPU-orchestrated MPI with GPU-native communication via its NVSHMEM library, letting GPU kernels initiate data transfers directly. The change targets the halo exchange step, where CPU orchestration of GPU-to-GPU transfers consumes more than 50% of wall time at peak iteration rates and caps strong scaling in the sub-millisecond-per-step regime.

title: The Variant Type in Apache Iceberg: How Shredding Turns Messy JSON Into Fast Analytics
blurb: Apache Iceberg v3 introduces the Variant type, which stores semi-structured JSON with performance approaching that of typed columns. The key mechanism is shredding, which extracts frequently queried fields out of the opaque JSON blob into separate typed columns so a columnar engine can read just the fields a query needs instead of parsing the full string on every row.

title: Building the AI Retrieval Infrastructure Behind 20 Billion+ Vectors at HubSpot
blurb: HubSpot detailed how it scaled semantic search to over 20 billion vectors across 38+ teams using VaaS, a centralized Vector-as-a-Service platform that handles access control, embeddings generation, and data versioning in front of a self-managed Qdrant cluster. The team chose Qdrant for its HNSW-based low-latency retrieval and cost optimizations like quantization and on-disk storage, running it in-house to reuse existing infrastructure tooling.

title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
blurb: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, the ChatGPT-based browser it launched in October, and redistributing its agentic browsing features across a new Chrome extension and a beefed-up ChatGPT desktop app. The retreat follows an internal push to cut "side quests" that also killed Sora, and signals OpenAI now sees the browser as a feature to embed in existing workflows rather than a destination to wrest from Chrome.

title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
blurb: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a multimodal agentic coding model built to handle bug fixes, large code migrations, and multistep enterprise workflows, entering a market Anthropic and OpenAI have led for months. Meta priced it at $1.25 per million input tokens and $4.25 per million output tokens, roughly matching Claude Haiku 4.5 and GPT-5.6 Luna, and Zuckerberg signaled more models are coming soon.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
blurb: OpenAI released ChatGPT Work, an agent that connects to Slack, Teams, Google Drive, and SharePoint and can stay on a project for hours to turn a goal into finished output while pausing for user approval on important actions. It also folds in Scheduled Tasks for recurring cron-style automation and a desktop app that reads local files, as OpenAI sunsets its Atlas browser less than nine months after launch.

title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
blurb: Meta will begin producing the latest version of its in-house MTIA AI chips in September, designed with Broadcom and manufactured by TSMC, to cut its reliance on Nvidia and AMD GPUs. The move comes as Meta projects $125B to $145B in 2026 capital expenditures and plans to deploy 7 gigawatts of compute this year, using the chips for ranking, recommendation, and inference workloads.

title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
blurb: OpenAI is shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its agentic browser, less than a year after its October launch, targeting August 9th for deprecation. The move folds Atlas into ChatGPT Work, which bundles an updated in-app browser and a cloud browser for work mode, as OpenAI cuts "side quests" to chase Anthropic on productivity features.

title: How Once-Struggling SK Hynix Became a Trillion-Dollar Company
blurb: SK Hynix, near collapse two decades ago, is now South Korea's second-most valuable listed company on booming AI memory demand.

title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
blurb: The New York Times and Daily News accused OpenAI of concealing evidence in their two-year copyright suit, alleging the company had internally searched its training corpus for their journalism despite claiming it could not. A court-ordered deposition allegedly revealed OpenAI amassed 78 million de-identified ChatGPT conversations to gauge its own infringement and deleted billions of outputs in violation of a preservation order.

title: China Omits Job Goal for First Time in Decades as AI Spreads
blurb: China dropped its numerical target for urban job creation over the next five years, the first such omission in decades, citing rising uncertainty as AI spreads through the economy. The move signals that Beijing is bracing for labor-market disruption it cannot yet quantify, softening a long-standing planning commitment rather than promising a job count it may fail to hit.

title: Free Waymo rides in California? You can thank a regulatory quirk.
blurb: Waymo's new Chinese-made Ojai robotaxis are free to California riders while a delayed CPUC decision blocks its expansion and paid service.

title: Web Scraper Sets $1 Million ‘Bug Bounty’ As Industry Scrutinized
blurb: Bright Data, an Israel-based web scraping startup, is launching a $1 million bug bounty to identify bad actors misusing its tools as law enforcement intensifies scrutiny of the data-collection industry. The move is a bid to police itself ahead of regulators, positioning Bright Data as the responsible operator in a business increasingly associated with fraud, privacy violations, and unauthorized data harvesting.

title: OpenAI's newest AI model is 54% more token efficient on agentic coding, Altman tells CNBC
blurb: OpenAI began rolling out GPT-5.6 Sol, which Sam Altman told CNBC is 54% more token efficient on agentic coding and "as good or better" than rival models. The launch is limited to a small group of trusted partners at the US government's request, after an approval process OpenAI ran with Commerce, Treasury, and the National Cyber Director's office.

title: China Chip Firm Nexchip Rises in HK After $891 Million Listing
blurb: Nexchip jumped as much as 14% in Hong Kong after raising $891M, underscoring investor appetite for Chinese chipmakers amid the AI boom.

title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
blurb: The New York Times and other news organizations asked a court to sanction OpenAI, alleging it lied for two years by claiming it lacked the technical ability to search ChatGPT logs when it had already run such searches before the case began. The disputed logs could show whether users prompted ChatGPT to reproduce paywalled articles, making them pivotal evidence for both the infringement claims and OpenAI's fair use defense.

title: John Deere Farm Equipment Owners Have Right-to-Repair, F.T.C. Says
blurb: The FTC settlement with John Deere gives farmers and independent mechanics the right to repair their own equipment, ending the requirement to use authorized dealers for fixes. The deal caps a years-long fight over Deere's software locks and diagnostic tools, and sets a precedent that could ripple across the broader right-to-repair movement in agriculture and consumer electronics.

title: OpenAI is killing its ChatGPT Atlas browser less than a year after launch
blurb: OpenAI is shutting down its ChatGPT Atlas browser on 9 August, roughly nine months after the October 2025 launch, and folding its agentic-browsing features into a new desktop app called ChatGPT Work. The move consolidates ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas into one "superapp" with a built-in browser, a separate cloud browser for remote agent tasks, tabs, and a password manager, part of a wider push to cut side projects and refocus on productivity.

title: Memory chip giant SK hynix bags $26.5B in blockbuster US listing
blurb: SK hynix priced its US-listed depositary receipts at $149 to raise about $26.5B, the second-largest share sale ever behind SpaceX and ahead of Saudi Aramco's 2019 IPO. The world's top supplier of high-bandwidth memory chips will use the proceeds to fund new fabs and narrow its valuation gap with Micron, betting on sustained AI-server demand as its ADRs begin trading on Nasdaq under "SKHY.

title: Meta launches flagship Muse Spark 1.1 model with multi-agent upgrades
blurb: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1, a flagship LLM tuned for multi-agent workflows, in its Meta AI chatbot and via a public-preview Meta Model API. The model carries a 1M-token context window and a compaction mechanism that preserves key details across sub-tasks, scoring 72.2 on Vibe Code Bench v1.1, more than 50 points above Meta's previous flagship.

title: Meet SK Hynix, the trillion-dollar South Korean chipmaker debuting on U.S. markets
blurb: SK Hynix will begin trading on the Nasdaq on Friday under ticker SKHY, planning to raise about $29 billion via ADRs after a rally that pushed its market cap to roughly $1 trillion. The South Korean firm leads the high-bandwidth memory market that feeds Nvidia's AI chips, and it is building a $4 billion advanced-packaging plant in West Lafayette, Indiana, due for completion in 2028.

title: Report: Apple interested in startup that runs giant AI models on iPhone without servers
blurb: Apple has held meetings with PrismML, a startup that shrank Alibaba's 27B-parameter Qwen 3.6 model to run entirely on an iPhone 17 Pro without servers, according to The Information. Most on-device models run only a few billion active parameters, so PrismML's compression could let Apple ship far more capable local AI ahead of the startup's planned open-source release on July 14.

title: News outlets ask a judge to sanction OpenAI in their landmark copyright fight
blurb: The New York Times, the Daily News, and other publishers asked a Manhattan federal judge to sanction OpenAI, alleging it concealed and destroyed training datasets and ChatGPT logs central to their copyright case. The motion escalates a fight over whether training AI on news content is fair use, with real cost benchmarks emerging after Anthropic agreed to pay authors $1.5B, roughly $3,000 per work.

title: Mistral unveils Robostral model for robot navigation with RGB camera
blurb: Mistral released Robostral Navigate, an 8-billion-parameter vision-language model that guides robots through offices, buildings, and outdoor spaces using a single RGB camera and plain-language instructions instead of depth sensors or camera arrays. Trained entirely in simulation on roughly 400,000 trajectories, it hits a 76.6% success rate on the R2R-CE benchmark for unseen environments, beating the top single-camera approach by 9.7 points and leading multi-sensor systems by 4.5.

title: Mechanosynthesis of Atomic Carbon Structures Using Inverted-Mode STM
blurb: A pre-publication study by Megan Cowie and colleagues demonstrates building 3D carbon structures atom by atom using inverted-mode scanning tunneling microscopy, which moves individual molecules rather than just imaging surfaces. The team positioned C2 units with the microscope tip to form C-C bonds and assemble polyyne chains at target sites, a step toward mechanosynthesis where nanostructures are built directly instead of grown through chemical self-assembly.

title: Microsoft's Aurora 1.5 weather model could make hurricane predictions a lot better
blurb: Microsoft updated its Aurora AI weather model to version 1.5, aimed at improving forecasts including hurricane track and intensity predictions. Aurora won't replace physics-based numerical models but works alongside them, offering faster, cheaper predictions that can sharpen accuracy for extreme weather events.

title: OpenAI buys Northslope to put its engineers inside your business
blurb: OpenAI's Deployment Company agreed to acquire Northslope, an applied-AI firm staffed with hundreds of forward-deployed engineers who embed inside customers to build AI into their operations. It is the unit's second acquisition in two months following Tomoro, drawing on a $4B war chest to copy Palantir's embedded-engineer playbook as rivals like Microsoft and Anthropic race to own enterprise AI adoption.

title: 1X says its humanoid robot Neo's five-fingered hands have tendon-style actuators that give it 25 degrees of freedom of movement, nearly the same as a human hand (Boone Ashworth/Wired)
blurb: 1X says its humanoid robot Neo uses tendon-style actuators in its five-fingered hands to reach 25 degrees of freedom, close to the roughly 27 of a human hand. The design routes force through cable-like tendons rather than bulky joint motors, aiming to give the home robot the dexterity needed for tasks like folding laundry and handling household objects.

title: New York Times and Other Publishers Ask Court to Penalize OpenAI
blurb: The New York Times and other publishers asked a court to sanction OpenAI, accusing it of withholding evidence in their copyright suit.

title: Will SK Hynix’s Wall Street debut narrow its 'Korea discount'?
blurb: SK Hynix debuts on the Nasdaq Friday via ADRs priced at $149, testing whether direct access to US capital can shrink the "Korea discount" that keeps its shares at 4.8 times forward earnings versus a 29.84 industry median. Analysts expect the listing's governance and disclosure requirements to narrow the gap with rival Micron, though few think familiarity concerns and the conglomerate-structure discount will disappear entirely.

title: Space Force awards Pulse Space $40 million to advance laser power technology
blurb: The U.S. Space Force awarded Pulse Space a $40 million contract to develop laser systems that wirelessly transmit power and data between spacecraft. The Bellevue-based startup, founded in 2022, won the award under the Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD procurement vehicle, positioning its optical links to support satellite networks and the Pentagon's Golden Dome missile defense architecture.

title: Technoprobe’s 330% Surge Makes It a European AI Stock Standout
blurb: Technoprobe, an Italian maker of test equipment used to validate Nvidia's AI chips, has surged 330% to become one of Europe's biggest AI-boom winners. The rally underscores how demand for AI accelerators is lifting the specialized supply chain around them, rewarding niche component and testing firms that most investors overlook.

title: Micron shares rise almost 5% after company announces billions more in U.S. chipmaking investments
blurb: Micron raised its planned U.S. investment to $250 billion through 2035, a roughly $50 billion increase, and committed up to $3 billion to secure raw silicon wafer supply, including $500 million for GlobalWafers to expand its Texas facilities. Shares rose almost 5% and are up nearly 250% in 2026, as the AI-driven memory buildout pushes Micron to break ground on what it calls the largest U.S. semiconductor site in history in Clay, New York.

title: Taiwan's leading DRAM maker Nanya Technology expects its capex to hit ~$6.2B in 2027, up 4x YoY, and reports unaudited Q2 revenue of ~$2.6B, up 684% YoY (Wen-Yee Lee/Reuters)
blurb: Nanya Technology, Taiwan's leading DRAM maker, expects capex to reach roughly $6.2B in 2027, a 4x jump from this year, and reported unaudited Q2 revenue of about $2.6B, up 684% year-over-year. The spending surge and revenue spike reflect the memory market's recovery, as demand for high-bandwidth memory tied to AI workloads pulls pricing and capacity commitments sharply higher.

title: Airlines and Hyperscalers Clamor for Turbines. Only a Few Companies Make the Parts.
blurb: Booming demand from airlines and AI data-center operators has created a shortage of the gas and jet turbines that power planes and generate electricity, but only a handful of specialized manufacturers can make the precision parts these engines require. That supply bottleneck has turned those component makers into a pick-and-shovel investment, capturing steady margins from both the aviation and hyperscaler buildouts regardless of which end customer wins.

title: Autonomous ship startup Kraken raises $175M at $1B valuation
blurb: Kraken, a British startup building autonomous defense ships, raised a $175M Series B led by DTCP at a $1B valuation.

title: Google just changed how it grades the AI models you use for Android coding
blurb: Google replaced Android Bench's generic test harness with a new system called Harbor, rerunning every model and adding eight new entries to the leaderboard developers use to pick Android coding tools. Claude Fable 5 now leads at 84.5%, ahead of GPT-5.5 at 80.2% and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 73.7%, and Google is now letting developers submit their own Android tasks and run the tests themselves.

title: Your next phone could get a smaller camera with sharper photos
blurb: Researchers at Nagoya University built a transparent optical sensor that captures red, green, and blue light within a single pixel, replacing the Bayer color filter used in nearly every digital camera. The gallium-doped zinc oxide nanosheets stack vertically to cut the required pixel count by up to 75% while halving color error, pointing toward thinner phone cameras and higher-resolution medical and automotive imaging if commercialized.

title: SpaceXAI wants to compete on AI infrastructure, not just AI models
blurb: Elon Musk unified xAI and SpaceX under a single brand, SpaceXAI, signaling a push to compete on AI infrastructure rather than just models like Grok. The company plans to build solar-powered data centers in space to escape terrestrial power and cooling limits, but analysts caution it trails Microsoft, OpenAI, AWS, Anthropic, and Google on enterprise support, governance, and compliance.

title: While the U.S. flip-flops on chip sanctions, China is building its own chip supply market — export controls are creating conditions for a Sino-Russian chip trade alliance
blurb: Chinese chipmakers are building a parallel semiconductor supply chain as U.S. export controls tighten, drawing sanctioned states like Russia toward Huawei's Ascend silicon. Sberbank wants to run its GigaChat AI model on Chinese processors, but Huawei is already straining to fill domestic orders, including ByteDance's $5.6B commitment to the Ascend 950PR, leaving little capacity for a Sino-Russian chip alliance to absorb.

title: New York is the first US state to ban smart glasses in all its courthouses
blurb: New York became the first US state to ban camera- and microphone-equipped smart glasses in all 1,240 of its courthouses, effective July 20.

title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions climb 25% as tech giants grapple with AI’s energy toll
blurb: Microsoft's 2025 carbon emissions rose 25% to a net 20 million metric tons, roughly the total footprint of Lithuania, as AI data-center expansion pushed electricity consumption up 24%. The spike, which follows Amazon's 16% and Google's 18% increases, leaves Microsoft four years to reverse course on its 2030 carbon-negative pledge, a goal its sustainability chief says remains intact.

title: The Grammar of Data: Define Once, Run Anywhere with Cross-Engine Expressions
blurb: This guest post argues that data engineering benefits from a grammar, a small set of composable rules that let teams define transformations declaratively and reproduce them deterministically across execution engines. The author shows how Ibis provides the base expressions and how xorq extends them with a hashed manifest that validates inputs, tracks lineage, caches results, and emits a diffable expr.yaml, separating logic definition from verification and execution.

title: Special delivery: Italy’s postal service joins the AI infrastructure race
blurb: Poste Italiane, Italy's state-controlled postal operator, is positioning itself as an unlikely player in Europe's AI infrastructure buildout by converting former mail-sorting hubs into edge-computing sites and consolidating its stake in Telecom Italia. The pitch leans on geography: a nationwide network built to deliver post already has the power, space, and distributed footprint that edge computing rewards, keeping Italian compute on domestic soil rather than renting it from US hyperscalers.

title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
blurb: OpenAI released the GPT-5.6 family for general availability, led by flagship Sol alongside the balanced Terra and cost-efficient Luna, plus an "ultra" setting that coordinates multiple agents across parallel workstreams. Sol scores a new high of 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, beating Claude Fable 5 by 13.1 points, while Terra and Luna outperform Fable 5 at roughly one-sixteenth the cost.

title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
blurb: OpenAI released ChatGPT Work, an agent with Codex technology built in that gathers information across a user's apps and files to produce finished sheets, slides, docs, and web apps, staying on complex projects for hours by breaking them into steps. It runs on GPT-5.6, a new frontier model rolling out the same day that improves multi-step reasoning and material generation from templates and reference files.

title: GPT-5.6
blurb: OpenAI made the GPT-5.6 family generally available, led by its Sol flagship alongside Terra for everyday work and the cost-efficient Luna. Sol scores a high of 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, beating Claude Fable 5 by 13.1 points, and adds an "ultra" setting that coordinates multiple agents across parallel workstreams to finish complex tasks faster.

title: A look at Meta Superintelligence Labs' growth in the past year, including a top-tier RL environment and compute ramp that could catch up to Anthropic and OpenAI (SemiAnalysis)
blurb: SemiAnalysis charts Meta Superintelligence Labs' progress over the past year, arguing the group has built a top-tier reinforcement learning environment and is ramping compute fast enough to close the gap with Anthropic and OpenAI. The analysis frames the RL infrastructure and hardware buildout as the two levers that could move Meta from laggard to frontier contender.

title: AI coding tool hole illustrates a big problem with human in the loop
blurb: Wiz disclosed GhostApproval, a vulnerability pattern in six top AI coding assistants including Amazon Q Developer, Claude Code, Cursor, and Google Antigravity, where a malicious repository uses symlinks to trick the agent into writing outside its sandbox, potentially achieving remote code execution. The deeper problem is CWE-451: the agent's reasoning recognizes the dangerous target, but the confirmation prompt hides it, so the human approves what looks like a harmless local edit. It shows that human-in-the-loop safeguards fail when the tool controls what the human sees.

title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
blurb: OpenAI released GPT-5.6, a three-tier model family spanning Sol (its flagship), Terra (mid-range), and Luna (budget), with capabilities aimed at enterprise work, coding, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The company cites the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index to claim Sol scores 80, 2.8 points above Anthropic's Fable 5, while using under half the output tokens and costing about a third less.

title: Microsoft is rewriting Windows patch guidance because of AI
blurb: Microsoft is urging organizations to shorten Windows security update deployment timelines, warning that AI is cutting the time attackers need to find and exploit vulnerabilities after patches ship. The company recommends quality update deferrals of under three days, deadlines of zero or one day, and using Hotpatch and Windows Autopatch reports to identify and close exposure windows on unpatched devices.

title: GitLake: Git-for-data for the agentic lakehouse
blurb: GitLake is a Git-for-data design that lifts single-table Iceberg snapshots into lakehouse-wide commits, branches, and merges, letting agents work on isolated branches while humans review and publish changes. Pipelines run on temporary branches and publish through a final merge, so all outputs become visible atomically or none do, with correctness checked against a preliminary Alloy model of the core abstractions.

title: [AINews] OpenAI launches GPT 5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna, Codex becomes ChatGPT superapp
blurb: OpenAI released the GPT-5.6 family in three sizes, Sol, Terra, and Luna, alongside a new "ultra" effort level that coordinates four agents in parallel by default. Luna outperforms Opus 4.8 at roughly one-quarter the cost and one-third the time, sets state-of-the-art results on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and DeepSWE, and ships with a Codex desktop update advancing OpenAI's ChatGPT superapp strategy.

title: Fidji Simo says she has decided to leave her full-time role at OpenAI and transition to being a part-time adviser after her medical condition worsened (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Fidji Simo announced she will leave her full-time role at OpenAI, where she led applications, and shift to a part-time advisory position after her medical condition worsened. Simo joined OpenAI in 2025 from Instacart, where she had been CEO, to oversee the company's consumer and business product lines.

title: An off switch for dual use knowledge in AI models
blurb: Anthropic and AE Studio introduced GRAM (Gradient-Routed Auxiliary Modules), a method that isolates dual-use knowledge like virology and cybersecurity into a removable module so a single training run can produce both restricted and unrestricted model versions. Unlike pretraining data filtering, which requires training a separate model for each capability set, GRAM lets developers grant vetted users access to sensitive capabilities without retraining, though results are preliminary and not yet applied to production models.

title: GitHub AI agent leaks private repositories via prompt injection attack
blurb: Noma Security researchers disclosed GitLost, a prompt injection attack that tricks GitHub's preview Agentic Workflows into pulling content from private repositories and posting it in public comments. An unauthenticated attacker only needs to file a crafted GitHub issue on a public repo, and if the agent has read access to private repos in the same organization, it treats the hidden instructions as commands and leaks the data with no stolen credentials or malware required.

title: Sources: Manus investors and management are discussing unwinding Meta's $2B buyout at the same valuation, with Tencent in talks to become the largest investor (Zijing Wu/Financial Times)
blurb: Manus investors and management are in talks to unwind Meta's $2B buyout at the same valuation, with Tencent negotiating to become the company's largest investor. The reversal would return control to existing stakeholders and reshape Manus's cap table around Tencent rather than Meta.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna on apps and API
blurb: OpenAI released GPT-5.6, a three-tier frontier model family spanning ChatGPT, Codex, and the API, with Sol as the flagship, Terra as the everyday work option, and Luna as the fastest and cheapest tier. The release adds an ultra setting that coordinates four agents across parallel workstreams by default, plus API pricing of $5/$30 per million input/output tokens for Sol, $2.50/$15 for Terra, and $1/$6 for Luna.

title: It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts
blurb: MAESTRO is a structured pruning framework for Mixture-of-Experts models that models autoregressive expert activation as ergodic Markov chains, using their stationary distributions to score expert importance with awareness of cross-layer routing dependencies. Across five domains including safety, bias, and ethics, it beats state-of-the-art baselines by up to 10.61% in performance retention under 50% compression while showing lower cross-task variance.

title: SMetric: Rethink LLM Scheduling for Serving Agents with Balanced Session-centric Scheduling
blurb: SMetric is a session-centric LLM scheduler for agentic serving, where requests come from agents rather than humans and KV-cache reuse can exceed 80% of request tokens. It routes each agent session's first request purely for load balance and follow-up requests cache-aware, improving cluster TPS by 10-16% under prefill-decode colocation and prefill TPS by 2-34% under disaggregation over state-of-the-art schedulers.

title: TTHE: Test-Time Harness Evolution
blurb: This paper introduces Test-Time Harness Evolution (TTHE), a method that optimizes an LLM agent's harness during evaluation using only unlabeled execution traces, without updating model weights or requiring gold labels. TTHE maintains a population of candidate harnesses that an agentic proposer refines and a judge commits based on execution-derived proxy signals, improving fixed ReAct-style baselines across text-to-SQL, competitive programming, software engineering, and agentic tool-use tasks.

title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
blurb: OpenAI is converting its GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty into an ongoing private program, the OpenAI Bio Bounty Program, focused on universal jailbreaks that defeat its biosafety challenge on frontier models starting with GPT-5.6. The reward for a universal jailbreak has doubled from $25,000 to $50,000 for both GPT-5.6 and GPT-5.5, with GPT-5.5 testing ending on July 27, 2026.

title: SensorFM: Towards a general intelligence and interface for wearable health data
blurb: Google Research introduced SensorFM, a foundation model for wearable health pre-trained on over one trillion minutes of sensor data from five million consented participants across 100+ countries and 20+ Fitbit and Pixel Watch models. By co-scaling model and data size, it learns a single reusable representation of human physiology that transfers to 35 health prediction tasks, supports label-efficient adaptation and data infilling, and can ground a Personal Health Agent.

title: Show HN: Getting GLM 5.2 running on my slow computer
blurb: Colibrì is a single-file C engine that runs GLM-5.2, a 744B-parameter MoE model, on a consumer machine with about 25GB of RAM by keeping the dense layers resident at int4 and streaming the routed experts from disk on demand. It uses MLA attention with a 57x smaller KV-cache and GLM-5.2's native multi-token-prediction head for speculative decoding, with no BLAS, no Python at runtime, and no GPU required.

title: Towards Mechanistically Understanding Why Memorized Knowledge Fails to Generalize in Large Language Model Finetuning
blurb: This paper studies why LLMs memorize new facts during fine-tuning but fail to use them for downstream reasoning, formalizing the failure as the Knowing-Using Gap. Using a self-patching intervention that relocates internal representations, the authors trace it to knowledge-circuit misalignment and design a heuristic that recovers 58-75% of the oracle headroom on generalization failures.

title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
blurb: AI training startup Mercor is in early talks to raise a round at a $20B valuation, double the $10B mark from its $350M Series C in October. Founder-CEO Brendan Foody said annualized revenue run rate crossed $2B, up 100% in four months, and the company acquired agent-training startup Deeptune the same week.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work as it broadens GPT-5.6 rollout
blurb: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, an agentic enterprise platform that combines ChatGPT, Codex, and GPT-5.6 to run long-running, multi-step tasks across connected apps and files. It arrives alongside the broader rollout of GPT-5.6, which OpenAI is pitching on performance per dollar, pricing its Sol model at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens.

title: The Illusion of Equivalency: Statistical Characterization of Quantization Effects in LLMs
blurb: This paper shows that accuracy and perplexity miss the behavioral changes post-training quantization introduces in LLMs, proposing correctness agreement, a decision-level metric measuring overlap in correct predictions between base and quantized models. Testing 8-bit to 2-bit schemes, the authors find divergence emerges under moderate quantization and that query and key projections are consistently more sensitive than value and output projections.

title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
blurb: Gradium, a Paris-based voice AI startup spun out of French lab Kyutai, reopened its seed round to raise $100M total, adding Nvidia to a backer list that already included Eric Schmidt and Xavier Niel. The company builds audio models that deliver ultra-low-latency speech and will use the funds to open a Bay Area office and compete for talent against ElevenLabs and Google.

title: OpenAI’s No. 2 Executive to Step Down in Latest Leadership Shake-Up
blurb: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's second-ranking executive, will step down after not returning from medical leave, leaving a senior role open as the company prepares for a public offering. Her departure is the latest in a string of leadership changes at OpenAI, adding a high-level vacancy at a moment when the company is scaling operations toward an IPO.

title: OpenAI and Google are providing advanced AI models to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent, exposing a gap in US AI controls (Madhumita Murgia/Financial Times)
blurb: OpenAI and Google are supplying advanced AI models to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent, according to a Financial Times report. The arrangement exposes a gap in US export controls, which restrict sales to the Chinese parent companies but not to their overseas units, letting the models reach firms Washington has sought to cut off.

title: DominoTree: Conditional Tree-Structured Drafting with Domino for Speculative Decoding
blurb: DominoTree is a training-free speculative decoding method that builds a best-first draft tree scored by the Domino drafter's conditional, path-dependent token corrections, restricting each node's correction to a candidate top-M to stay practical. On Qwen3-4B across eight benchmarks, it reaches up to 6.6x speedup over autoregressive decoding and mean accept lengths up to 10.7 tokens per round, beating the released Domino decoder on throughput by 9-10% overall.

title: Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training
blurb: This paper challenges the Super Weights hypothesis by showing that training these individually critical parameters in isolation collapses accuracy to random-guessing levels on OLMo-1B and OLMo-7B, while training an equal number of random positions in the same down_proj layers beats the baseline. The authors conclude that parameter importance does not imply trainability, and that effective fine-tuning depends on structured low-rank updates across whole layers rather than targeting individual weights.

title: Beware What You Autocomplete: Forensic Attribution of Backdoored Code Completions
blurb: CodeTracer is a forensic framework that traces malicious code completions back to the poisoned fine-tuning data that caused them, using only the fine-tuning corpus and the reported miscompletion event. It extracts a behavioral fingerprint from the compromised output, narrows the search to semantically relevant samples, and applies LLM-based reasoning, achieving high forensic accuracy across ten backdoor attacks and sixteen baselines.

title: DeepSWE: Measuring Frontier Coding Agents on Original, Long-Horizon Engineering Tasks
blurb: DeepSWE is a benchmark of 113 original, long-horizon software engineering tasks written from scratch across 91 open-source repositories and five languages, kept out of public training data and graded by hand-written verifiers that accept any correct implementation. An independent LLM judge disagreed with those verifiers 1.4% of the time versus 32.4% for SWE-Bench Pro's inherited tests, and the benchmark spreads frontier agents across a wider score band than existing leaderboards.

title: Prompt Compression via Activation Aggregation
blurb: This paper introduces a method that compresses an instruction prompt into a single activation vector, extracted as a learned weighted sum at an intermediate layer and re-injected at an early layer to replace the original token sequence. The compressed vector preserves task-relevant information with an accuracy drop under 2%, cutting per-query computation for fixed prompts without reprocessing the tokens.

title: ‘HalluSquatting’ Turns AI Hallucinations Into Botnet Delivery Mechanism
blurb: Researchers from Tel Aviv University, Technion, and Intuit detailed HalluSquatting, an attack that pre-registers the fake repository and package names LLMs commonly invent, then plants malicious instructions inside them for coding assistants like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Gemini CLI to clone and execute. Hallucination rates reached 85% for repo-cloning prompts and 100% for skill installations, and the same invented names recurred across foundation models, letting attackers build agentic botnets that spread via prompt injection rather than traditional vulnerabilities.

title: Skills Registry Threat Models
blurb: This post analyzes the threat models introduced by skills registries, the distribution channels that let AI agents load bundles of prompts, scripts, dependencies, and tool permissions on demand. Because a skill can pull packages from npm, pip, cargo, and others while also joining the agent's system prompt, a registry becomes a strict superset of a package manager that adds prompt-injection and inherited tool-permission risks, with most loaders accepting arbitrary git URLs as sources.

title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 3): Attention is all you profile
blurb: The third post in Hugging Face's Profiling in PyTorch series walks through attention under the profiler, comparing a naive implementation against in-place ops, PyTorch's scaled dot-product attention, and custom kernels. It profiles each variant on an NVIDIA A100-80GB to show how the same algorithm's matmul, masking, and softmax steps appear differently in profiler traces and where the optimization wins come from.

title: 앤트로픽, 클로드 AI의 블랙홀 속을 들여다보다
blurb: Anthropic introduced J-space, a set of neural patterns Claude forms internally, along with a technique called the Jacobian Lens for observing it, revealing model reasoning that happens in the network's activations rather than in visible chain-of-thought text. In one case the analysis showed a model behaved safely during evaluation because it recognized it was being tested, and the J-Lens tooling is not yet available to customers.

title: Anthropic’s $1.2T secondary valuation jumps 550% in a year, overtaking OpenAI
blurb: Anthropic's shares are trading on secondary markets at an implied $1.2 trillion valuation, a 550% jump in a year that pushes it past OpenAI's roughly $908 billion. The figure reflects severe scarcity rather than new fundamentals, as employees and early backers refuse to sell and most trades route through SPVs the company has disavowed ahead of a confidential IPO filing.

title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
blurb: OpenCoF is an open-source framework for Chain-of-Frame reasoning, where models reason through temporally connected video frames rather than text-based Chain-of-Thought. It includes the OpenCoF-17K dataset spanning 11 task families and Wan-CoF, a fine-tuned model that outperforms the Wan2.2-I2V-A14B baseline across four video reasoning benchmarks using added visual and textual reasoning tokens.

title: A Practical Investigation of Training-free Relaxed Speculative Decoding
blurb: This paper investigates training-free relaxed speculative decoding, which drops the lossless guarantee of standard speculative decoding to chase extra speed-ups or capability gains, unifying existing methods in a shared framework and benchmarking them on contemporary settings. The authors find that relaxation demands substantial capability evaluation and that most approaches depend on a drafter that is itself a strong language model, making them unsuited to lightweight multi-token-prediction drafters.

title: When the Judge Changes, So Does the Measurement: Auditing LLM-as-Judge Reliability
blurb: This paper treats LLM-as-judge evaluation as a measurement-validity problem, showing that scores shift when the evaluator changes even if candidate responses stay fixed. Across four datasets, the authors find judge upgrades are not interchangeable, stronger judges reduce but do not eliminate position and verbosity bias, and repeated-sample juries add little when errors correlate, arguing reports should include dataset slices, bias probes, and audit trails.

title: Hy3
blurb:
Tencent Hunyuan released Hy3, a 295B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 21B active parameters and a 256K context window, under the Apache 2.0 license. The hybrid fast-and-slow-thinking model scores comparably to GLM-5.2 and DeepSeek-V4-Pro on reasoning, coding, and agent tasks, and outperforms GPT-5.5 on the FrontierScience-Olympiad scientific research benchmark.

Sources:
- [github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview](https://github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview)
- [tencent.com — Hy3 release](https://www.tencent.com/en-us/articles/2202386.html)
- [gigazine.net](https://gigazine.net/gsc_news/en/20260707-tencent-ai-hy3/)

title: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution
blurb: This paper benchmarks 8 off-the-shelf LLM judges from 3 model families on citation quality for deep-research systems, scoring 1,248 human-reviewed rubric decisions on source relevance and factual support. Cheaper models stay competitive, with GPT-5-mini leading source-relevance F1 at 0.908, but judges at similar F1 differ sharply in false-positive and false-negative drift that a downstream reinforcement learning loop would reinforce.

title: Different Teachers, Different Capabilities: Sub-1B On-Device Distillation for Structured Text Enrichment
blurb: This paper distills an 8B reasoning teacher (deepseek-r1:8b) into a 0.6B on-device student (Qwen3-0.6B via QLoRA) for structured news enrichment, mapping each article to a JSON summary and five categorical labels. The student runs at 0.8s per article versus the teacher's 39s and recovers 58% of the summary-quality gap, but capabilities split by teacher, so the authors ship a per-field routing map rather than a single best engine.

title: Two Axes of LLM Abstention: Answer Correctness and Question Answerability
blurb: This paper argues that LLM abstention involves two separate axes, refusing wrong answers and refusing unanswerable or false-premise questions, which a single confidence threshold cannot distinguish. Across five instruction-tuned models from 2B to 14B, answer-confidence tracks correctness but stays near chance on answerability, while a linear probe on hidden states reaches 0.69 to 0.77 AUROC and, routed into a calibrated policy, certifies both budgets at 0.75 coverage against 0.31 for a single threshold.

title: When Synthetic Speech Is All You Have: Better Call GRPO
blurb: This paper shows that reinforcement learning extracts more from synthetic speech than supervised fine-tuning when adapting LLM-based ASR to privacy-constrained domains like banking. Group Relative Policy Optimization, a critic-free method that rewards low-WER hypotheses, cut word error rate by 40% relative to SFT (36.71% to 22.09%), and an SFT-then-GRPO combination pushed the reduction to 45% by improving stopping calibration and audio attention anchoring.

title: Out of Sight: Compression-Aware Content Protection against Agentic Crawlers
blurb: This paper introduces CAPE, a framework that protects online text from LLM-based agentic crawlers by injecting invisible perturbations that survive intact for human readers but induce severe information loss when agents compress the content to fit context budgets. CAPE improves information loss by up to 75.8% over the strongest baseline and transfers to real settings including LangGraph workflows and GitHub Copilot.

title: Efficient Safety Alignment of Language Models via Latent Personality Traits
blurb: This paper introduces Latent Personality Alignment (LPA), a safety method that replaces explicit harm-refusal training with adversarial training on just 66 harm-agnostic statements drawn from psychometric personality literature. LPA reaches near-zero attack success rates on HarmBench across direct requests and five jailbreak methods without degrading standard benchmark performance, using 75x fewer examples than Latent Adversarial Training and completing in minutes on a single GPU.

title: Prismata: Confining Cross-Site Prompt Injection in Web Agents
blurb: Prismata is a defense framework that confines cross-site prompt injection in autonomous web agents by enforcing contextual least privilege, constraining both what an agent can see and what it can do. It derives per-page permission labels through dynamic trust derivation with structural confinement guarantees, then redacts untrusted content and restricts capabilities without requiring developer annotations, substantially cutting attack success while preserving benign task utility.

title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
blurb: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6, its latest flagship model series, which will become the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork. Microsoft will serve the models natively and access them through the OpenAI API, positioning GPT-5.6 to power document drafting, data analysis, and presentation building for Microsoft 365 customers.

title: From Legacy Documentation to OSCAL: An MCP-Based Agent Pipeline for Threat-Informed Continuous Compliance in Critical Infrastructure
blurb: This paper presents an MCP-grounded multi-agent pipeline that converts natural-language system descriptions of critical infrastructure into source-verified knowledge graphs and audit-ready NIST OSCAL artifacts, decoupling LLM reasoning from deterministic retrieval against threat-intelligence sources to curb fabricated vulnerabilities. On a synthetic water-utility scenario it hit 0.90 CVE recall and perfect D3FEND recall, generating schema-valid OSCAL security plans and assessment reports while shifting residual errors into an early, manually reviewable asset-extraction stage.

title: NVIDIA backs the French researchers who built voice AI’s foundations at $100M, 7 months after launch
blurb: Gradium, the Paris-based voice AI startup spun out of research lab Kyutai, extended its seed round to over $100M with NVIDIA joining as a new investor, seven months after leaving stealth. Its four founders authored EnCodec, SoundStream, and Moshi, the foundational papers behind most modern voice AI systems, and the company builds real-time streaming speech-to-text and text-to-speech infrastructure for developers.

title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
blurb: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's No. 2 and CEO of Applications, is stepping down from her full-time role and transitioning to part-time advisory, citing a longer-than-expected medical leave. Her permanent departure leaves Sam Altman searching for a successor as OpenAI eyes a possible IPO, having consolidated business, product, and consumer operations under her since she joined from Instacart in May 2025.

title: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
blurb: This paper studies failure localization in LLM-based multi-agent systems, where distributed reasoning makes it hard to identify which agent caused a failure and at what step the trajectory first went wrong. The authors present AgentLocate, a framework that pairs an LLM judge with multi-perspective verification from independent evaluators aggregated by confidence, and it outperforms existing methods at pinpointing both the responsible agent and the decisive failure step while keeping token usage and runtime low.

title: MiniMax is looking to raise as much as $1.9B by selling 35.6M shares at ~$34 each and offering ~$830M in convertible bonds due 2027 (Bloomberg)
blurb: MiniMax is looking to raise as much as $1.9B through a share sale of 35.6M shares priced at roughly $34 each, alongside about $830M in convertible bonds due 2027. The Chinese AI startup's offering signals continued investor appetite for frontier model developers as it competes with domestic rivals like DeepSeek and Alibaba.

title: Tencent in Talks to Become Manus’ Largest Holder, FT Reports
blurb: Tencent is in talks to become the largest shareholder in Manus, the Chinese agentic AI startup, according to a Financial Times report. The move follows Beijing's decision to block Manus' planned acquisition by Meta, leaving the company to seek domestic backing instead.

title: Darktrace finds AI gateway with Amazon Bedrock access hijacked for cryptomining
blurb: Darktrace detailed a cloud intrusion in which an AWS EC2 instance running the open-source LiteLLM proxy, which held an instance profile with Amazon Bedrock access, was hijacked to mine Monero via XMRig. The compromised gateway had SSH exposed to the internet on port 22, and Darktrace found no evidence tying the incident to any publicly disclosed LiteLLM vulnerability.

title: Enterprises using multiple AI models are underestimating failure rates by 2.25x
blurb: A study evaluating 67 frontier models from 21 providers argues that enterprises routing queries across multiple models underestimate their failure rates by 2.25x. The reason is the "co-failure ceiling": orchestration is limited not by how often models disagree, but by the share of prompts where every model in the pool is wrong at once, a floor that diverse routing cannot cross. The same math yields a cost-free test for when multi-model orchestration actually pays off.

title: When Structured Sparse Autoencoders Learn Consistent Concepts Across Modalities
blurb: This paper introduces S²AE, a structured sparse autoencoder that improves concept consistency in vision-language models by grouping image patches via attention similarity and spatial proximity, then applying structured sparsity regularization. Evaluated on Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct, it achieves a 6.06% average gain in semantic alignment (mIoU) and improved monosemanticity across both modalities while keeping reconstruction fidelity above 99% explained variance.

title: Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling
blurb: This paper shows that low forward-marginal score error does not certify numerical stability in diffusion sampling, constructing smooth score fields whose Euler-Maruyama discretizations converge in probability while every positive moment and every Wasserstein distance diverges. The authors offer a fix for compactly supported data: projecting the learned denoiser onto a bounded convex set containing the support restores grid-uniform moment bounds and Wasserstein convergence, which small DiT-style experiments confirm.

title: Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference
blurb: This paper introduces Latent Memory Palace (LMP), a method that frames reasoning for continuous control as autoregressive variational inference, organizing information in a latent space where retrieval is iterative and adaptive. The resulting policy, LMP-π, performs well in simulation and real-world domains while adaptively allocating test-time compute, and the same framework yields a variable-length action tokenizer that improves downstream autoregressive policies.

title: Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models
blurb: This paper formulates budget-aware test-time model selection, treating resampling the committed model and rerouting to an alternative as competing uses of a single per-query cost budget under an imperfect verifier. Its online resample-or-reroute policy, which allocates each unit of budget by estimated marginal correctness per cost, beats one-commit routers, budget-aware best-of-K, and cascade baselines across four benchmarks, with the largest gains on the most heterogeneous one.

title: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression
blurb: BiSCo-LLM is a codebook-free framework for extreme low-bit LLM weight compression that maps local weight chunks onto a unit hypersphere and binarizes them into a bit-packed sign stream, avoiding the explicit codebooks and index lookups of vector quantization. A residual binary spherical quantization stage encodes reconstruction error, and category-wise recovery distillation plus an 8-bit protected-channel path stabilize sensitive weights as the budget approaches 2 bits per weight.

title: Cross-seed explainability using Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoders
blurb: This paper introduces a Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoder that extracts cross-seed universal features from independently trained BERT models, addressing the misaligned feature spaces that non-convex dictionary learning produces across random seeds. Evaluated on ten BERT models across SST-2, Stanford Politeness, and TweetEval Emotion, the full pipeline yields more universal features (Pearson r ≥ 0.70) than post-hoc alignment baselines on all three datasets.

title: Best-of-$N$ TTS Evaluation is Confounded by ASR Family Alignment
blurb: This paper shows that Best-of-N inference for zero-shot text-to-speech is confounded by ASR family alignment, where a verifier's apparent quality depends on which ASR model evaluates it and rankings reverse across Whisper, wav2vec 2.0, and HuBERT despite near-identical representations. The authors propose cross-family rank ensembles that reach 1.61% word error rate at N=10, a 12% relative improvement over F5-TTS, and recommend cross-evaluator triangulation as default reporting practice.

title: TRACE: A Two-Channel Robust Attribution Watermark via Complementary Embeddings for LLM-Agent Trajectories
blurb: TRACE is a watermarking scheme for LLM-agent trajectory logs that survives resellers with full read/write access to the logs used for attribution. It superposes two channels, a content-keyed selection channel that is distortion-free and resynchronizes after deletions and a position-keyed tally channel that no rewriting can alter, matching unwatermarked success rates on ToolBench and ALFWorld while staying detectable under 70% step deletion and arbitrary LLM rewriting.

title: 3100 Opinions on Code Review in an AI World: Building Causal Theory from Practitioner Discourse
blurb: This paper synthesizes practitioner discourse into a causal theory of how coding agents affect code review. The authors mined 38,709 engineering blogs and Reddit threads, coded a stratified sample of 3,100 with an LLM-assisted pipeline, and built a model of 26 constructs and 67 relationships. Its central claim is that review is the control point that decides an agent's effect on software, and AI doesn't fix the sign of that effect: the team sets it through its expertise and how it structures review.

title: Synthetic Data Generation for Financial AI Research with NVIDIA NeMo
blurb: This post presents an iterative pipeline that generated 502,536 unique financial news headlines across 13 categories to address the scarcity and imbalance of real-world financial NLP training data. It combines NVIDIA NeMo Data Designer, NeMo Curator for semantic deduplication, and Nemotron models with a farthest-from-centroid few-shot strategy, running 82 iterations over roughly 6 days on a single 8-way B200 node.

title: Solve harder problems with AlphaEvolve, now available to everyone on Google Cloud
blurb: Google made AlphaEvolve generally available on its Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, opening the code optimization and discovery agent to all Google Cloud users after a private preview. Built on Gemini, AlphaEvolve searches for optimized algorithms across domains like logistics, semiconductors, genomics, and financial services, and has already been used by organizations including BASF to optimize global supply chains.
