The move expands Tether's regional footprint in a market where USDT already dominates dollar-denominated payments and remittances, and gives Mercado Bitcoin capital to grow tokenization and settlement products for Brazilian users.

title: New Hampshire snuffs out trailblazing state-government bitcoin bond effort
blurb: New Hampshire's Executive Council rejected the Business Finance Authority's plan to issue a rated, bitcoin-backed bond, killing the project in a 3-2 vote at its final approval stage. The instrument would have backed a private-sector bond of up to $100M tied to Bitcoin miner CleanSpark and carried a Ba2 rating from Moody's, marking the first bitcoin-backed bond issued under a US state's authority.

title: White House defends Trump’s regulatory appointments as CFTC vacancies complicate crypto bill push
blurb: The White House sent a letter to Senate leaders Thune and Schumer defending its regulatory appointments, after Democrats blamed the administration's failure to fill vacancies for stalling the Clarity Act, the crypto market structure bill. The dispute centers on the CFTC, which would oversee digital assets under the bill but currently has just one of five commissioner seats filled, Republican Chair Michael Selig.

title: JPMorgan says bitcoin’s main risk isn’t Strategy, but blockchain adoption that doesn’t benefit public chains and tokens
blurb: JPMorgan analysts led by Nikolaos Panigirtzoglou said the main structural risk to bitcoin is not Strategy's BTC sales but blockchain adoption that bypasses public permissionless networks. If tokenization, payments, and settlement keep migrating to permissioned bank-built infrastructure over chains like Ethereum, the analysts warn the broader crypto ecosystem could face a structural de-rating with lower liquidity and weaker capital flows, eventually weighing on BTC.

title: Summer.fi Hacker Moves $1.35M Into Tornado Cash
blurb: The attacker behind the $6.04M Summer.fi exploit began laundering the proceeds, routing roughly $1.35M in DAI through the sanctioned Tornado Cash mixer via an intermediary wallet after swapping to ETH on Uniswap, per Summer.fi's July 6 post-mortem. About $4.67M in DAI still sits untouched in the exploiter's primary wallet, and tracing breaks down once funds enter the mixer.

title: Ethereum's newest nonprofit wants to become Wall Street's guide to crypto
blurb: Ethereum Institutional, a nonprofit launched last week and led by former Ethereum Foundation enterprise staff David Walsh, Matthew Dawson, and Marius Smith, aims to serve as a neutral guide helping banks and asset managers navigate the Ethereum ecosystem. The group says it has built roughly 500 institutional relationships over the past year and will connect enterprises with developers and infrastructure providers without promoting any single product.

title: Hyperliquid, Phantom Ask CFTC to Exempt DeFi From Broker Rules
blurb: Hyperliquid's policy arm and wallet provider Phantom filed a joint comment asking the CFTC to exempt onchain protocol software and non-custodial wallets from exchange and broker registration rules. Their most concrete ask is to codify the no-action relief the CFTC granted Phantom in March into a formal rule covering all non-custodial wallet providers, testing how far Chairman Michael Selig will extend the agency's crypto-friendly stance.

title: Fiserv explores sale of debit card network to big US banks
blurb: Fiserv is exploring a sale of its Accel debit card network to a consortium of large US banks including JPMorgan and Bank of America, according to the WSJ. A bank-owned debit network would give the group a domestic alternative to Visa and Mastercard, cutting interchange costs and tightening control over payment rails that route transactions today.

title: Stablecoins under Stress in a National Economy: Transaction-Level Evidence from Austrian Crypto-Asset Service Providers
blurb: Using a regulatory registry that identifies the on-chain addresses of every crypto-asset service provider registered in Austria, the authors reconstruct roughly $30B in Bitcoin, Ether, USDC, and USDT flows and split them into retail-like and institutional activity. During the Terra-Luna, FTX, and SVB shocks, the two groups moved through different mechanisms, and stablecoins did not behave as a uniform safe haven. The clearest case is SVB, where retail deposits and institutional withdrawals track USDC's two-tiered redemption design, patterns invisible in aggregate data.

title: Supreme Court ruling expanding Trump’s authority over federal agencies raises questions for SEC, CFTC as crypto rulemaking advances
blurb: The Supreme Court ruled 6–3 that President Trump can remove leaders of most independent federal agencies except the Federal Reserve, giving the White House greater sway over the SEC and CFTC as both write new crypto rules. The timing matters because the CFTC is down to a single commissioner and the SEC holds three Republicans, leaving digital-asset rulemaking to thinner, more partisan panels that critics say produce less durable regulation.

title: Tarun Chitra’s Gauntlet raises $125 million Series C from sole investor SBI Holdings
blurb: Gauntlet, a crypto vault curator, raised a $125M Series C from Japan's SBI Holdings as sole investor, its second such deal this week after backing EDX Markets' $76M round. Gauntlet, which curates over $1.5B across DeFi vaults for 150-plus institutions, will use the capital to add peso-, yen-, and other fiat-backed stablecoin coverage beyond USD and EUR.

title: UK’s Lendable Raises Debt Backed by Personal-Loan Portfolio
blurb: Lendable is raising £500M ($671M) through a securitization backed by a portfolio of its personal loans, funding to expand its consumer-lending business. The deal gives the UK digital lender a capital-markets funding source beyond deposits and equity, a common step as fintech lenders scale origination and seek to lower their cost of capital.

title: PayPal's PYUSD Goes Native on Polygon, Joins Open Money Stack
blurb: PayPal's PYUSD is now issued natively on Polygon by Paxos and integrated into the network's Open Money Stack, which bundles payins, payouts, wallets, and cross-border orchestration behind a single payments API. Native minting rather than bridging is the bet, though Polygon currently holds just $10.13M, or 0.4% of PYUSD's $2.83B supply, making the move a play for future distribution over existing volume.

title: Polymarket seeks approval to bring margin trading to U.S. customers
blurb: Polymarket's U.S. affiliate applied for a National Futures Association futures commission merchant license to offer margin trading, which would let American users take positions without fully collateralizing them, and it still needs CFTC approval to change its rulebook. The move follows Kalshi's March clearance for margin and comes as prediction-market volumes track toward roughly $240B in 2026, up from $51B last year.

title: Newest version of crypto Clarity Act may drop as soon as next week, sources say
blurb: Senate lawmakers may release a merged version of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act as soon as next week, combining the Banking and Agriculture Committees' crypto market-structure work into one text reported to have grown by more than 70 pages. The draft still lacks the Democratic buy-in needed for 60 Senate votes, with ethics limits on officials' crypto business ties as the main unresolved sticking point.

title: JPMorgan Enters Vault Curation With $700M USDC Vault
blurb: JPMorgan launched a $700M tokenized vault denominated in USDC through its Kinexys division, running on Ethereum and investing in U.S. Treasury bills, bonds, and overnight repos. The debut instantly makes JPMorgan the fifth-largest onchain vault curator, a notable move for a bank whose CEO Jamie Dimon has repeatedly dismissed stablecoins and DeFi.

title: Polymarket files applications to offer regulated margin trading in US
blurb: Polymarket filed applications with the National Futures Association to offer regulated margin trading in the US, seeking futures commission merchant registration through affiliate PM Derivatives before it can enable leveraged bets with CFTC sign-off. The move trails rival Kalshi, whose affiliate secured NFA approval as a registered FCM in March, as both platforms posted record June volumes of $33B and roughly $14B respectively.

title: Synthetic Data Generation for Financial AI Research with NVIDIA NeMo
blurb: NVIDIA published a walkthrough of an iterative NeMo pipeline that generated 502,536 unique financial news headlines across 13 categories, using NeMo Data Designer for structured generation, NeMo Curator for semantic deduplication, and Nemotron models for synthesis. The workflow targets the class imbalance in real financial feeds, oversampling rare events like credit-rating changes and product approvals to fine-tune NLP models for trading research, risk modeling, and surveillance.

title: Aave Labs Launches Stable Vaults for Fintech Stablecoin Yield
blurb: Aave Labs launched Stable Vaults, infrastructure that lets fintechs, wallets, exchanges, and payment providers embed fixed-rate stablecoin yield into their own products by converting variable Aave V3 and V4 lending rates into a fixed rate each business sets for its users. Operators choose the accepted stablecoins, strategies, and rate, keeping any yield earned above the promised rate as revenue, turning the vaults into an on-chain fixed-income product rather than a floating-rate pass-through.

title: How to Scale a Neobank from 0 to 60,000 Customers
blurb: The author walks through how they scaled a neobank from zero to 60,000 customers, breaking down the acquisition tactics, onboarding funnel, and sponsor-bank economics that made unit costs work at each stage of growth. The thread argues that early neobank traction hinges less on product features than on nailing a narrow customer segment and the interchange math behind it before spending on scale.

title: Grounded Event Extraction from SEC 8-K Filings with a Fine-Grained Taxonomy
blurb: This paper presents a two-stage LLM system that tags 292,984 SEC 8-K filings from 2022 to 2026 against a 119-type event taxonomy, anchoring every label to a verbatim quote and re-grading it for a calibrated quality score. Across 5,125 sampled tags, precision climbs from 12% to 96% as the score rises, and an event study on abnormal returns confirms the taxonomy separates market-moving disclosures that share the same coarse SEC item code. The authors release all 601,088 grounded event tags.

title: Coinbase obtains MiFID license in the United Kingdom
blurb: Coinbase secured FCA authorisation to provide investment services in the UK, letting it offer derivatives and equities alongside crypto under a single MiFID-equivalent license. Retail users can trade equities on Coinbase for the first time, and the permission sits alongside the firm's existing e-money license and crypto registration, advancing its push to consolidate banking, brokerage, and crypto into one platform.

title: Revolut integrates its crypto exchange with AI assistants as agentic trading spreads
blurb: Revolut connected its standalone crypto exchange, Revolut X, to third-party AI assistants including Claude, Gemini, and Cursor, letting customers analyze markets, set price alerts, and place market and limit orders through natural-language prompts. Users must review and approve every order, and Revolut disclaims liability for erroneous trades, keeping the human in the loop as agentic trading spreads across crypto firms serving its 16 million crypto users.

title: HSBC Issues First Digitally Native Structured Product in Hong Kong
blurb: HSBC issued its first digitally native structured product in Hong Kong, a USD-denominated private placement of notes minted directly on a blockchain rather than tokenized after issuance, with Euroclear-backed Marketnode acting as tokenization and digital paying agent. The bank withheld the issuance size, reference asset, tenor, and chain, framing the deal as a pilot to test how tokenization streamlines issuance, settlement, and lifecycle servicing for institutional structured notes.

title: Filing: Polymarket is seeking CFTC approval to offer margin trading in the US, a move that would let users bet on events with less capital upfront (Bloomberg)
blurb: Polymarket filed for CFTC approval to offer margin trading in the US, which would let users place event-based bets while posting less capital upfront. The request pushes prediction markets closer to the leverage mechanics of regulated derivatives, and CFTC sign-off would set a precedent for how much risk retail traders can take on event contracts.

title: Payment Firms Emerge as Target in Fight Against Illegal Casinos
blurb: The European Gaming and Betting Association filed a complaint with Lithuania's central bank seeking an investigation of payment firm Walletto, alleging it processes transactions for unlicensed online casinos. The move signals a shift in enforcement toward payment intermediaries rather than the operators themselves, putting sponsor-bank and PSP compliance teams on notice that servicing gambling merchants now carries direct regulatory exposure.

title: Market data platform startup Databento closes $97M round after drawing $300M in investor demand
blurb: Databento raised $97M in a round led by New Enterprise Associates, drawing over $300M in investor demand, to expand its market-data coverage across asset classes and push into more than 20 exchange data centers. The startup captures futures, options, and equities data at the source and sells it on demand through one API, targeting the procurement-heavy incumbency of Bloomberg and London Stock Exchange Group.

title: Hong Kong SFC orders crypto platforms, online brokers to phase out OTP logins
blurb: Hong Kong's SFC ordered licensed crypto platforms and online brokers to phase out one-time passwords for customer login and device binding within 12 months, mandating passkeys and device binding instead. The move follows a surge in account takeovers, with spoofing attacks accounting for 57% of reported security incidents in 2025, and requires firms to add surveillance of suspicious login, trading, and withdrawal activity.

title: Paris-based Aria raises €7 million and launches €240 million debt facility to tackle Europe’s late-payment crisis
blurb: Aria raised a €7M Series A extension led by 115K, La Banque Postale's venture arm, and launched a €240M securitisation facility to fund its embedded invoice-financing platform across Europe. The bankruptcy-remote vehicle, led by Nomura, buys supplier invoices and issues investor-backed securities against buyers' future payments, targeting a late-payment problem the EU Payment Observatory estimates locks up over €100B in annual cash flow.

title: PayPal seeks to increase use of PYUSD stablecoin with native issuance on Polygon
blurb: PayPal will now issue its PYUSD stablecoin natively on Polygon and make it available through Polygon's Open Money Stack, letting businesses take in, move across borders, and cash out payments in a single integration with compliance built in. The move targets adoption for a token with roughly $3B in supply, still far behind Tether and Circle, on a network that has settled over $2.6T in stablecoin transactions.

title: Privy Launches Global Fiat Onramps With Stripe in US, EU
blurb: Privy launched global fiat onramps that let developers add card-based crypto purchases through a single API, with Stripe's Crypto Onramp handling payments, KYC, and compliance in the US and EU. Outside those regions, Privy's own aggregator routes users across 100+ countries to the same destination wallet, marking the first major product from the wallet-infra provider since Stripe acquired it in 2025.

title: EU Banks Urged to Pool Buying Power in Deals With US Tech Giants
blurb: Dutch officials proposed that European banks and finance firms pool their buying power when negotiating with US tech giants, part of a push to reduce the continent's dependence on foreign providers of AI and cloud infrastructure. The proposals reflect growing regulatory anxiety in Europe over critical fintech services concentrated in a handful of American vendors, and they signal potential collective-procurement pressure on incumbents like AWS, Microsoft, and Google.

title: SK Hynix Makes Biggest Foreign Debut in US
blurb: SK Hynix raised $26.5B in an American depositary receipt offering, the largest US first-time share sale ever by a foreign company. The South Korean memory chipmaker gains a deep US fundraising channel and gives investors another vehicle to bet on AI infrastructure, pulling capital-markets demand for AI exposure further into the hardware supply chain rather than the model layer.

title: Former Chase UK boss joins Revolut to lead European entity
blurb: Revolut appointed Kuba Fast, the former CEO of Chase UK, to lead Revolut Bank UAB, its Lithuania-based entity licensed by the European Central Bank and the Bank of Lithuania. The hire signals Revolut's push to scale banking operations across the EEA, where a full banking license lets it hold deposits and lend directly rather than relying on partner arrangements.

title: Challenger banks: What the sell-side is telling us
blurb: Neobank profitability is being validated by the sell-side, with Autonomous Research's Josh Levin arguing that Nubank, Revolut, and peers have reached "institutional adulthood" by shifting from growth-at-all-costs to profitability and embracing the compliance they once resisted. Levin's data upends the high-ARPU playbook: the most profitable digital banks monetize tens of millions of low-ARPU customers efficiently rather than extracting maximum value from smaller, high-value bases.

title: Tether Invests $20M in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin
blurb: Tether invested $20M in Mercado Bitcoin, one of Brazil's largest crypto exchanges, backing the platform's expansion into tokenized assets and stablecoin distribution across Latin America. The stake deepens Tether's push to embed USDT into local payment and settlement rails in high-inflation markets, where dollar-pegged stablecoins increasingly function as everyday transaction infrastructure rather than a trading instrument.

title: The Hidden Economics of Payment Success Rates
blurb: The article reframes payment success rates as a core business metric rather than an engineering concern, arguing that small authorization gains compound into meaningful revenue across millions of transactions. Each failed payment carries costs beyond the lost sale: support load, cart abandonment, weaker retention, and involuntary churn for subscription businesses. Failures stem from a mix of issuer declines, expired credentials, authentication friction, and fraud rules, making success-rate optimization a commercial lever, not just a technical one.

title: PayPal joins the European Payments Council
blurb: PayPal joined the European Payments Council, gaining a seat among the banks and payment providers that govern SEPA credit transfer and direct debit schemes across Europe. Membership positions PayPal to participate in the SEPA Instant rollout and the rulemaking around it, deepening the platform's reach into euro-denominated account-to-account rails rather than relying solely on card networks.

title: Alipay upgrades Tap! devices for agentic commerce
blurb: Alipay upgraded its Tap! in-store devices to support agentic commerce, letting AI agents complete purchases and loyalty actions when a user taps their phone at checkout. The move extends Ant Group's NFC tap-to-pay hardware beyond human-initiated payments, positioning Alipay's merchant terminals as a settlement point for AI agents transacting on a shopper's behalf across its retail network.

title: Aave rolls out vaults for yield-hungry fintech investors
blurb: Aave Labs launched Stable Vaults, a product that lets wallets, exchanges, and payment apps embed stablecoin yield on USDC, USDT, and GHO through a single integration, with deposits auto-allocated across approved DeFi lending strategies. The move puts Aave against Morpho, whose vaults already power Coinbase's USDC savings product and a similar Robinhood offering, as fintechs race to monetize idle stablecoin balances.

title: Robinhood's market structure ambitions move beyond brokerage
blurb: Robinhood spent the past 13 months assembling a full US market-structure stack, owning a crypto exchange via Bitstamp, a CFTC-licensed exchange and clearinghouse through Rothera and MIAXdx, banking with $2B in deposits, tokenized stocks on its own chain, and sole-trustee status for the Treasury's Trump Accounts. Owning settlement rather than just distribution shifts Robinhood's comp from Schwab toward ICE or Nasdaq, and concentrates exchange, clearing, and custody risk in one entity that regulators unwound after 2008.

title: The Hidden Risk in Neobank Earn Products
blurb: Neobanks and crypto wallets increasingly bolt on Earn products without a dedicated DeFi or risk owner, letting their BD team source whatever paid integrations they can close. The author argues most of those yields sit near 2% real APY, propped up by incentives, so advertising 6-7% forces a reach into riskier products. Once the incentives dry up, a fintech either drops to the DeFi base rate or splits off a separate high-yield account, because safety and high yield cannot coexist in one product.
